# Africa Tech Summit Nairobi 2025 Announces 10 Ventures for Investment Showcase
Lauren Adair
Share:
[ ](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.africatechsummit.com%2Finsights%2Fafrica-tech-summit-nairobi-2025-announces-10-ventures-for-investment-showcase%2F) [ ](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.africatechsummit.com%2Finsights%2Fafrica-tech-summit-nairobi-2025-announces-10-ventures-for-investment-showcase%2F&text=Africa%20Tech%20Summit%20Nairobi%202025%20Announces%2010%20Ventures%20for%20Investment%20Showcase) [ ](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.africatechsummit.com%2Finsights%2Fafrica-tech-summit-nairobi-2025-announces-10-ventures-for-investment-showcase%2F) [ ](https://wa.me/?text=Africa%20Tech%20Summit%20Nairobi%202025%20Announces%2010%20Ventures%20for%20Investment%20Showcase%20https%3A%2F%2Fwww.africatechsummit.com%2Finsights%2Fafrica-tech-summit-nairobi-2025-announces-10-ventures-for-investment-showcase%2F)
January 16, 2025 Time to read: 2 minutes
Africa Tech Summit Nairobi powered by Raenest, the leading B2B event connecting the African tech ecosystem, is excited to announce the 10 African tech ventures selected to showcase their innovative solutions for potential funding and partnerships on February 12th and 13th at the Sarit Expo Centre in Nairobi.
Africa Tech Summit Nairobi 2025 Announces 10 Ventures for Investment Showcase – Over 200 submissions were received from more than 24 countries, including Burundi, Côte d’Ivoire, Democratic Republic of Congo, Egypt, Ethiopia, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, Ghana, Zambia, Mali, Morocco, Mozambique, South Africa, Tanzania, Chad, and Rwanda. The 10 ventures, representing sectors such as fintech, insurtech, healthtech, agritech, and cleantech, were chosen for their innovative ideas and potential to drive significant impact across the continent.
Recently released data from the startup funding tracker [Africa: The Big Deal](https://thebigdeal.substack.com/p/2024sum) reveals that fintech accounted for nearly half of all startup funding in 2024. Among the four largest fundraisers was Moniepoint, which secured $110 million in a Series C round, valuing the company at over $1 billion and making it Africa’s eighth Unicorn. While the African startup ecosystem has experienced a slump in recent years, funding activities are projected to recover in 2025, driven by innovative startups and growing investor interest.
The selected ventures will present their solutions to industry leaders, entrepreneurs, and global investors. They Include:
[Bizy Tech Limited (Tanzania)](https://bizytech.com/) developed a digital agricultural platform, KILIMO BaNDO, to support smallholder farmers and agro-small and medium enterprises by providing access to essential agricultural services and financial solutions.
[Dojah (Nigeria)](https://dojah.io/) is an anti-fraud infrastructure platform that enables businesses to detect, prevent, and manage fraudulent activities in real-time.
[Duhqa (Kenya)](https://www.duhqa.com/) is a B2B platform that connects retailers with manufacturers and suppliers, locally and internationally. They aim to solve logistics and delivery inefficiencies by enabling retailers to get their goods delivered conveniently and affordably.
[Flow Global (Uganda)](https://www.flowglobal.net/our-company) is addressing the significant problem faced by Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in developing countries: the lack of access to adequate financing that hinders their growth potential.
[Heala Tech Ltd (Nigeria)](https://heala.ng/) is a digital health platform that streamlines healthcare delivery in Africa, connecting patients, providers, and insurers through scalable tech solutions to improve access, affordability, and efficiency across the ecosystem.
[JaliKoi (Rwanda)](https://www.jalikoi.rw/) provides a simplified approach for low and middle-income earners to access financial services.
[Koolboks (France, Nigeria and Kenya)](https://www.koolboks.com/) provides pay-as-you-go (PAYG) solar-powered refrigeration solutions to underserved communities and SMEs, enabling 24/7 cold storage to reduce food waste and promote climate resilience.
[MaishaPoa (Kenya)](http://www.maishapoa.co.ke/) is an insurtech startup that provides affordable and accessible micro-insurance solutions, protecting low- and middle-income households from financial shocks.
[Pricepally (Nigeria)](https://www.pricepally.com/?srsltid=AfmBOorveClW8x61S3fv4T6xqkXTMeleITq77nBPa7h0x4VLRrIFcKLK) is an e-commerce startup addressing inefficiencies in the food distribution system.
[OnSpace Technologies (Kenya)](http://www.getonspace.com/) is a no-code platform that addresses a critical problem faced by agricultural SMEs and related industries: the lack of affordable, accessible, and adaptable business systems.
“We are delighted to welcome these ventures to the seventh edition of Africa Tech Summit (ATS) Nairobi,” said Andrew Fassnidge, Director of Africa Tech Summit. “The ATS Investment Showcase continues to showcase and connect investment ready tech ventures, with leading VC’s and support them in a challenging funding environment”.
For more information about the seventh edition of Africa Tech Summit Nairobi 2025 which is powered by Raenest and kindly supported byMoniepoint, Amazon Web Services, IFC, Circle, Andela, Tether, Conduit, Absa Bank, Celo, Opera, Mento Labs, PawaPay, GIZ, Deimos, SmileID, Equator VC, Mercy Corps Ventures, the UK Department for Business and Trade, London Stock Exchange, Fincra, International Trade Centre, Tala, Turaco, Remedial Health, Executives in Africa, Carnegie Mellon University, Fez Delivery, Tola Mobile, Datamellon, Tappi, and the Investment Showcase, please visit the [Africa Tech Summit](https://www.africatechsummit.com/nairobi/) website.

tavily.answer:
Africa Tech Ventures' official website is https://vc4a.com/africa-tech-ventures-atv. It focuses on EdTech, HealthTech, and ICT in Sub-Saharan Africa. Founded by experienced investors, it aims to accelerate tech-enabled business growth.
Africa Tech Ventures invests in scalable tech startups in Sub-Saharan Africa, focusing on growth and access to essential services. Founded by experienced investors, it provides equity and network support. As of 2026, it has backed over 180 companies across 25 countries.
Africa Tech Ventures focuses on funding scalable tech startups in Sub-Saharan Africa, investing in sectors like EdTech and HealthTech. The African tech ecosystem has seen significant growth, with venture capital funding increasing despite global challenges. Fintech remains a central pillar in Africa's tech sector.
Africa Tech Ventures is a prominent startup hub in Africa, focusing on tech and innovation, with notable companies like Lilt and TripSalama actively hiring. Lilt offers advanced translation services, while TripSalama provides travel solutions for Africa.
Africa Tech Ventures is a prominent player in African tech venture capital, focusing on high-growth startups. It organizes DealRoom events to connect African startups with investors. The firm has achieved notable success with multiple unicorns in its portfolio.
